title: podman auto-update in conflict with pods
body:
### Issue Description I'm using `podman auto-update` and I'm getting the error > auto-updating container "febf6...": no PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT label found. This only happens if I create the service with the option for the container to be inside a pod. When I remove the pod option, auto-update works without any major problems. Could someone help me understand the problem?
I'm using `podman version 4.9.4-rhel`
------ ### Steps to reproduce the issue To reproduce the error, I followed these steps: ````shell # Create Pod podman pod create \ -p 3050:3051 \ -p 5000:5001 \ --name pod-nginx # ------------------------------- # CHOOSE ONE TO RUN # Create container with Pod = ERROR podman run \ -d \ --restart=always \ --pod pod-nginx \ -v uptime-kuma:/app/data \ --name my-kuma \ --label "io.containers.autoupdate=local" \ docker.io/louislam/uptime-kuma:1 # Create container without Pod = NO ERROR podman run \ -d \ --restart=always \ -v uptime-kuma:/app/data \ --name my-kuma \ --label "io.containers.autoupdate=local" \ docker.io/louislam/uptime-kuma:1 # ------------------------------- # Create Service podman generate systemd --new my-kuma > ~/.config/systemd/user/my-kuma.service systemctl --user daemon-reload # Drop Docker Instance podman rm -f my-kuma # Start Service systemctl --user enable my-kuma.service systemctl --user restart my-kuma.service systemctl --user status my-kuma.service # Update podman auto-update ````
To clean the tests above... ````shell systemctl --user stop my-kuma.service systemctl --user daemon-reload rm ~/.config/systemd/user/my-kuma.service podman pod rm pod-nginx -f ````
------ ### Describe the results you received When I try the keep my podman updated inside a pod, I recive the error: `````shell podman auto-update UNIT CONTAINER IMAGE POLICY UPDATED my-kuma.service f6bcc5b898d6 (my-kuma) docker.io/louislam/uptime-kuma:1 local false Error: auto-updating container "febf6585f37d48871efb94803c3e49baa0d1a0fb1bcfaa3c0e93d972ae629913": no PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT label found ````` When I create a service (to update) without a pod (outside a pod) a receive no erros... ````shell podman auto-update UNIT CONTAINER IMAGE POLICY UPDATED my-kuma.service 6a9d04ada52d (my-kuma) docker.io/louislam/uptime-kuma:1 local false ````
------ ### Describe the results you expected I would like to know if there are any restrictions on using both services together: `podman auto-update` plus pods. ### podman info output host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.33.8 cgroupControllers: - memory - pids cgroupManager: systemd cgroupVersion: v2 conmon: package: conmon-2.1.10-1.el9.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.1.10, commit: fb8c4bf50dbc044a338137871b096eea8041a1fa' cpuUtilization: idlePercent: 99.7 systemPercent: 0.05 userPercent: 0.25 cpus: 4 databaseBackend: boltdb distribution: distribution: rhel version: "9.4" eventLogger: journald freeLocks: 1971 hostname: (i hide this) idMappings: gidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 165536 size: 65536 uidmap: - container_id: 0 host_id: 1001 size: 1 - container_id: 1 host_id: 165536 size: 65536 kernel: 5.14.0-427.33.1.el9_4.x86_64 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 1417818112 memTotal: 3798859776 networkBackend: netavark networkBackendInfo: backend: netavark dns: package: aardvark-dns-1.10.0-3.el9_4.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns version: aardvark-dns 1.10.0 package: netavark-1.10.3-1.el9.x86_64 path: /usr/libexec/podman/netavark version: netavark 1.10.3 ociRuntime: name: crun package: crun-1.14.3-1.el9.x86_64 path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 1.14.3 commit: 1961d211ba98f532ea52d2e80f4c20359f241a98 rundir: /run/user/1001/crun spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL os: linux pasta: executable: /usr/bin/pasta package: passt-0^20231204.gb86afe3-1.el9.x86_64 version: | pasta 0^20231204.gb86afe3-1.el9.x86_64 Copyright Red Hat GNU General Public License, version 2 or later This is free software: you are free to change and redistribute it. There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law. remoteSocket: exists: false path: /run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: true seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: true serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: slirp4netns-1.2.3-1.el9.x86_64 version: |- slirp4netns version 1.2.3 commit: c22fde291bb35b354e6ca44d13be181c76a0a432 libslirp: 4.4.0 SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3 libseccomp: 2.5.2 swapFree: 4099637248 swapTotal: 4198494208 uptime: 238h 17m 18.00s (Approximately 9.92 days) variant: "" plugins: authorization: null log: - k8s-file - none - passthrough - journald network: - bridge - macvlan - ipvlan volume: - local registries: search: - registry.access.redhat.com - registry.redhat.io - docker.io store: configFile: /home/michelsilva/.config/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 3 paused: 0 running: 3 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /home/michelsilva/.local/share/containers/storage graphRootAllocated: 33273937920 graphRootUsed: 9625772032 graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: xfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Supports shifting: "false" Supports volatile: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp imageStore: number: 13 runRoot: /run/user/1001/containers transientStore: false volumePath: /home/michelsilva/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 4.9.4-rhel Built: 1719829634 BuiltTime: Mon Jul 1 07:27:14 2024 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.21.11 (Red Hat 1.21.11-1.el9_4) Os: linux OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 4.9.4-rhel
------ ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Rootless ### Upstream Latest Release Yes
------ ### Additional information I'm trying to maintain services running in podmans, such as flask applications (python), FastAPI applications (python), static sites and uptime kuma, for example, in isolated podmans. Finally, I want an NGINX podman to expose all the services to the internet. I want the pod to "group" all these isolated podmans, however, I would like to be able to keep them updated using podman auto-update.

comments.body: You have to run the entire pod under systemd not just a single container, i.e. run `podman generate systemd` wiyth the pod name and the `--files` option to generate the files for the pod and container.

I really appreciate your help.

I used to create the service without the `--files` parameter to keep the names more readable and I tried, without success, to use this with the pods...

When I added the `--files` parameter and created the service for the pod (instead of the individual podmans) everything worked fine!

Now I have pods and podman-auto-update!
Tks!!!
